Implements a manufacturing button test application for Pebble devices, handling button presses and test results.

# Purpose
The code is a C program designed for a button testing application on a Pebble device. It manages a graphical user interface (GUI) that tests the functionality of the device's buttons. The program initializes a window with text layers for displaying the test status and title, and path layers for visual indicators corresponding to each button. The application tracks button presses using a bitset and updates the display based on the test's progress. If all buttons are pressed within a specified time, the test passes; otherwise, it fails. The test results are recorded if the manufacturing information recording feature is enabled.

The program includes several key components: `AppData` structure to store the application's state, functions to handle button clicks and time ticks, and initialization functions to set up the GUI and event handlers. The [`prv_handle_second_tick`](<#prv_handle_second_tick>) function updates the test status every second, while [`prv_button_click_handler`](<#prv_button_click_handler>) processes button presses. The [`prv_handle_init`](<#prv_handle_init>) function initializes the application, setting up the window and subscribing to the tick timer service. The [`s_main`](<#s_main>) function starts the application event loop, and [`mfg_button_app_get_info`](<#mfg_button_app_get_info>) provides metadata about the application, including its UUID and name.

# Data Structures

---
### AppData
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``window``: Holds the main application window.
    - ``arrows``: Array of `PathLayer` objects representing arrow graphics for each button.
    - ``buttons_pressed``: Bitset indicating which buttons have been pressed.
    - ``title``: Text layer for displaying the title of the application.
    - ``status``: Text layer for displaying the current status of the application.
    - ``status_string``: Character array for storing the status message.
    - ``seconds_remaining``: Countdown timer for the remaining seconds.
    - ``test_complete``: Boolean flag indicating if the test is complete.
- **Description**: `AppData` is a structure that encapsulates the state and UI components of an application, including a window, graphical elements for button indicators, and text layers for displaying the title and status. It also manages the state of button presses, a countdown timer, and a flag to indicate test completion.


# Functions

---
### prv\_handle\_second\_tick<!-- {{#callable:prv_handle_second_tick}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/prf_apps/mfg_button_app.c#L55>)

Handles the countdown and status update for a button test, determining if the test is complete and updating the display accordingly.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``tick_time``: A pointer to a `struct tm` representing the current time.
    - ``units_changed``: A `TimeUnits` value indicating which time units have changed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the user data using `app_state_get_user_data()` and store it in `data`.
    - Check if `data->test_complete` is true; if so, decrement `data->seconds_remaining` and pop the window if it reaches zero, then return.
    - Determine if the test is passed by checking if `data->buttons_pressed` equals `0x0f`.
    - If `data->seconds_remaining` is zero or the test is passed, set `data->test_complete` to true.
    - If `MFG_INFO_RECORDS_TEST_RESULTS` is defined, write the test result using `mfg_info_write_test_result()`.
    - Update `data->status_string` to "PASS!" or "FAIL!" based on the test result and set `data->seconds_remaining` to `WINDOW_POP_TIME_S`.
    - If the test is not complete, update `data->status_string` with the remaining time and decrement `data->seconds_remaining`.
    - Set the text of `data->status` to `data->status_string` using `text_layer_set_text()`.
- **Output**: No return value; updates the `status_string` and `status` text layer of the `AppData` structure.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`app_window_stack_pop`](<../../applib/ui/app_window_stack.c.md#app_window_stack_pop>)
    - [`sniprintf`](<../../../../tests/overrides/default/stdio.h.md#sniprintf>)
    - [`text_layer_set_text`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text>)


---
### prv\_button\_click\_handler<!-- {{#callable:prv_button_click_handler}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/prf_apps/mfg_button_app.c#L90>)

Handles button click events by updating the button state and managing the window visibility.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``recognizer``: A reference to the `ClickRecognizerRef` that identifies which button was clicked.
    - ``data``: A pointer to additional data, not used in this function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the application data using `app_state_get_user_data()` and store it in `app_data`.
    - Get the ID of the button that was pressed using `click_recognizer_get_button_id(recognizer)` and store it in `button_id_pressed`.
    - Update the `buttons_pressed` bitset in `app_data` to include the pressed button using `bitset32_set()`.
    - Hide the arrow layer corresponding to the pressed button by calling `layer_set_hidden()` with the appropriate layer.
    - Check if the test is complete by evaluating `app_data->test_complete`.
    - If the test is complete, remove the application window from the stack using `app_window_stack_remove()` with animation set to false.
- **Output**: No return value; the function operates by side effects on the application state and UI.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`bitset32_set`](<../../../../platform/snowy/boot/src/util/bitset.h.md#bitset32_set>)
    - [`layer_set_hidden`](<../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_hidden>)


---
### prv\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable:prv_config_provider}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/prf_apps/mfg_button_app.c#L102>)

Subscribes button click events to a handler function for all four buttons on a device.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``data``: A pointer to user-defined data, not used in this function.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Subscribes the `BUTTON_ID_BACK` button to the `prv_button_click_handler` function.
    - Subscribes the `BUTTON_ID_UP` button to the `prv_button_click_handler` function.
    - Subscribes the `BUTTON_ID_SELECT` button to the `prv_button_click_handler` function.
    - Subscribes the `BUTTON_ID_DOWN` button to the `prv_button_click_handler` function.
- **Output**: No return value.

### init\_arrow\_layer\_for\_button<!-- {{#callable:init_arrow_layer_for_button}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/prf_apps/mfg_button_app.c#L109>)

Initializes an arrow layer for a specified button in the application data.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``data``: A pointer to `AppData` structure containing application-specific data.
    - ``id``: The `ButtonId` representing the button for which the arrow layer is initialized.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Define static [`GPoint`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GPoint>) array `ARROW_PATH_POINTS` to specify the points of the arrow path.
    - Define `GPathInfo` structure `ARROW_PATH_INFO` using `ARROW_PATH_POINTS` to describe the arrow path.
    - Define static `GRect` array `ARROW_RECTS` to specify the frame for each button's arrow.
    - Get the `PathLayer` pointer `arrow` from `data->arrows` using `id`.
    - Initialize the `arrow` path layer with `ARROW_PATH_INFO`.
    - Set the fill and stroke color of `arrow` to `GColorBlack`.
    - Set the frame of `arrow->layer` using `ARROW_RECTS[id]`.
    - If `id` is `BUTTON_ID_BACK`, rotate the arrow path by 180 degrees and move it to a new position.
    - Add `arrow->layer` as a child to the window layer in `data`.
- **Output**: None (the function modifies the `AppData` structure in place).
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`path_layer_init`](<../../applib/ui/path_layer.c.md#path_layer_init>)
    - [`path_layer_set_fill_color`](<../../applib/ui/path_layer.c.md#path_layer_set_fill_color>)
    - [`path_layer_set_stroke_color`](<../../applib/ui/path_layer.c.md#path_layer_set_stroke_color>)
    - [`layer_set_frame`](<../../applib/ui/layer.c.md#layer_set_frame>)
    - [`gpath_rotate_to`](<../../applib/graphics/gpath.c.md#gpath_rotate_to>)
    - [`GPoint`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GPoint>)

### prv\_handle\_init<!-- {{#callable:prv_handle_init}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/prf_apps/mfg_button_app.c#L141>)

Initializes the application data and UI components for a button test.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Allocate memory for `AppData` and initialize its fields with default values.
    - Set the user data in the application state to the newly allocated `AppData`.
    - Initialize a `Window` and configure it to be fullscreen, override the back button, and set a click configuration provider.
    - Initialize a `TextLayer` for the title, set its font, alignment, and text, then add it to the window's layer.
    - Initialize a `TextLayer` for the status, set its font and alignment, then add it to the window's layer.
    - Iterate over each button ID and initialize an arrow layer for each button using [`init_arrow_layer_for_button`](<#init_arrow_layer_for_button>).
    - Push the window onto the application window stack with animation enabled.
    - Subscribe to the tick timer service to call `prv_handle_second_tick` every second.
- **Output**: No output is returned.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_init`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_init>)
    - [`window_set_fullscreen`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_fullscreen>)
    - [`window_set_overrides_back_button`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_overrides_back_button>)
    - [`window_set_click_config_provider`](<../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_click_config_provider>)
    - [`text_layer_init`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_init>)
    - [`text_layer_set_font`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_font>)
    - [`text_layer_set_text_alignment`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text_alignment>)
    - [`text_layer_set_text`](<../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_text>)
    - [`GRect`](<../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>)
    - [`init_arrow_layer_for_button`](<#init_arrow_layer_for_button>)

    - Invokes `app_event_loop` to start processing events.

### mfg\_button\_app\_get\_info<!-- {{#callable:mfg_button_app_get_info}} -->
[View Source â†’](<../../../../../../src/fw/apps/prf_apps/mfg_button_app.c#L187>)

Returns a pointer to a static `PebbleProcessMdSystem` structure containing metadata for the MfgButton application.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Defines a static `PebbleProcessMdSystem` structure `s_app_info` with application metadata.
    - Sets the `main_func` field to point to the `s_main` function.
    - Initializes the `uuid` field with a specific UUID value.
    - Sets the `name` field to "MfgButton".
    - Returns a pointer to the `s_app_info` structure.
- **Output**: A pointer to a `PebbleProcessMd` structure containing the application's metadata.
